library_preparation_preflight.summary: Meaningful jffi coverage requires the packaged native stub library; the main com.github.jnr:jffi:1.3.9 JAR has no runtime dependencies and does not contain the jni/ native resources, so native-backed APIs fail without the Maven native classifier on the runtime classpath.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Add the jffi native classifier to the test runtime classpath before exercising native-backed APIs, e.g. Gradle classifier notation `testRuntimeOnly("com.github.jnr:jffi:1.3.9:native")`. The useful tests should load/exercise real jffi APIs such as Type, Library, MemoryIO, Invoker, or closure/native stub behavior after the native stub is available. No Docker service is needed. If native loading is redirected manually, jffi also honors `jffi.boot.library.path`, `java.library.path`, `jffi.extract.dir`, and `jffi.extract.name`, but the normal path is to keep the native classifier JAR on the runtime classpath.
risks: The preflight deterministic dependency schema accepts only group:artifact:version coordinates, so it cannot directly express the required Maven classifier artifact.
Native-image verification may require metadata for loading/extracting the `jni/<platform>/libjffi-1.2.*` resource and using JNI/System.load.
Tests are platform-sensitive because jffi selects a native stub by OS/CPU and extracts it to a writable executable temp directory.
